commit.message:
Add launch session dialog with config-aware dropdowns (Phase 3.6) (#115)

## Summary
- Add "Launch Session" button and inline form to Project Detail page
- Task (required textarea), Role, Runtime, Isolation, and Merge Strategy
fields — all as dropdowns
- Dropdowns fetch actual project config defaults via `GET
/api/projects/:id/config` and show them as the default option
- Role dropdown populated from new `GET /api/roles` endpoint that lists
installed roles from `~/.strawpot/roles/`
- On submit, POSTs to `/sessions` and navigates to the new session
detail page
- Advanced options (Runtime, Isolation, Merge Strategy) hidden behind
toggle

## Test plan
- [x] `npm run build` passes
- [x] `python -m pytest tests/ -v` — 79 tests pass
- [ ] Verify Launch Session form appears on project detail page
- [ ] Verify dropdowns show config defaults and installed roles
- [ ] Verify session launch creates a new session and navigates to
detail

fix: non-user thread replies now trigger fork respawn when fork is dead (#1893)

When a coworker or channel lead posted a thread reply to a topic channel
where the fork session was dead, the nudge went to a dead session because
the non-user sender path didn't check fork liveness. Added the same
try_lazy_fork_respawn() call that the user message path already had.

commit.message:
Fix OOM: use sequential scan when gradient checkpointing

Parallel scan creates O(T*logT) intermediate tensors per layer.
Even with layer-level grad checkpointing, one layer's forward peak
is ~15GB at batch=8. Sequential scan uses O(T) memory instead.
With grad checkpointing + sequential scan, SSM memory drops from
~60GB to ~2GB per layer. Trade: ~30% slower scan, but fits on 80GB.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Bundle game data with executables instead of CDN downloads

Changed approach from auto-downloading from Project Gorgon CDN to bundling
data files with executables to respect server resources and ensure offline
functionality.

Changes:
- Add quests.json and items.json to repository (10.4MB total)
- Update PyInstaller builds to bundle data files with --add-data
- Add get_bundled_resource_dir() to config.py for PyInstaller detection
- Update data_updater.py to copy from bundle before attempting download
- Update web_server.py to use bundled resources when available
- Remove quests.json and items.json from .gitignore

Behavior:
- Executables now include game data (no CDN hits on first run)
- Falls back to CDN download only if bundled copy fails
- Running from source still uses local files if present
- Reduces load on Project Gorgon CDN
- Works offline after initial setup

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix manasight/manasight-docs#243: file rotation detection (#61)

Implement file rotation detection in FileTailer so that when MTGA
replaces Player.log on restart, the tailer automatically re-opens
the file and resumes parsing from the beginning.

Detection uses two signals:
- Primary: file size at path < current offset (file shrunk)
- Secondary: mtime jump >60s without new data at offset

On rotation: close old handle, re-open at path, reset offset to 0,
clear partial_line and line_buffer, emit LogFileRotated event.

Changes:
- tailer.rs: add check_rotation(), RotationInfo, take_rotation()
- events.rs: add LogFileRotated variant and LogFileRotatedEvent
- stream.rs: rotation-aware poll loop emits event to bus
- event_bus.rs: derive Clone for EventBus (needed by stream)
- lib.rs: re-export LogFileRotatedEvent

commit.message:
build: switch from uv_build to hatchling

uv_build couples the build-system version constraint to uv's release
cycle, requiring pyproject.toml edits on every minor uv upgrade.
hatchling is stable, PEP 517 compliant, and works with any build tool.

Adds [tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel] packages config for the src/
layout. Switching also removes uv_build's own dependencies
(contextvars-extras, dependency-injector, sentinel-value) from the
lockfile.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: python-lab
repository.description: A lab for Python code.

commit.message:
feat: simplify context system, remove webhooks, add complete_and_summarize

Major architectural changes:
- Remove webhooks entirely — synchronous ACK on message receipt instead
- Remove text shards — code shards only (live resolution, push-synced)
- Remove darkmatter_inbox, darkmatter_status, darkmatter_forward_message tools
- Merge forwarding into begin_message(forward_message_ids=[...])
- Replace trust_threshold with share_with_top_n (top-N peers by trust)
- Simplify context feed: last 20 messages chronologically (was scored ranking)
- Add darkmatter_complete_and_summarize: mandatory end-of-task digest that
  prunes raw session history, pushes summary to peers, spawns warm replacement
- Messages consumed from queue on sight (wait_for_message, spawn, context)
- Status line written to ~/.darkmatter/status.txt instead of MCP tool
- Summary entries are first-class context with 25k char / 5k word caps

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: pass full env and capture stderr for MCP server spawns

StdioClientTransport's default strips env to 6 vars on macOS, causing
gws mcp to exit immediately. Pass process.env so child processes inherit
the complete environment. Also pipe stderr and log it on connection
failure for diagnostics.

commit.message:
Add weekly scheduled notifications (top thought + absurd conversion)

Two cron jobs via node-cron: Weekly Top Thought (Sundays 9 PM ET) broadcasts
the most-upvoted mint of the week, and Weekly Absurd Conversion (Wednesdays
12 PM ET) sends a playful BTC conversion. Both use broadcastNotification
with deterministic notificationIds for Farcaster dedup. Top thought queries
MongoDB directly instead of scanning blocks.

Fix GPU/Storage UI freezing and monitoring issues

GPU fixes:
- start() now returns bool with error message via last_error()
- GpuPanel checks initialize() result, disables button if no GPU backend
- GpuPanel shows QMessageBox on start failure instead of silent fail
- Metrics callback invoked outside mutex lock to prevent worker blocking

Storage fixes:
- start() returns bool with error reporting
- stop() deletes test file in background thread (prevents UI freeze)
- File pre-creation shows "preparing" state with progress percentage
- Windows WriteFile/ReadFile error checking added
- Metrics callback actually invoked in all I/O loops
- State tracking: preparing -> testing -> completed/error

CLI fixes:
- GPU/Storage start() failures return exit code 2 with error JSON
